issue_body:
I've started to get junk mail starting with
```
Hello,

Your certificate (or certificates) for the names listed below will expire in 17 days (on 03 Mar 16 18:43 +0000). Please make sure to renew your certificate before then, or visitors to your website will encounter errors.
```
The problem is that this is about a certificate I regularly replace by auto-renewing it.  I'm not using the 2.5 month old version anymore but the replacement.

You shouldn't nag us about certs that have been replaced and no longer in use.

issue_title: Don't ask to temporarily shut down web server to pass challenge, instead make use of proxy_pass
issue_body:
I've recently set up letsencrypt on my server and I was not quite happy of how the default setup process works:

letsencrypt uses port 80 to do the challenge/response thing https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/236af19a50de18a7cd831157dc53a6d22f79ee10/acme/acme/challenges.py#L223
It asks to temporarily stop an already running web server for it to bind its own web server to port 80 https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/d209b5ea20c2f5f35eadc304dbe5cbb85c233f4d/letsencrypt/plugins/standalone.py#L222

This causes downtime of the main http service. Downtime = bad.
Please offer an option to bind to another port and proxy_pass it to port 80. This will not cause any downtime.

issue_title: Webroot map cannot be modified through the CLI during renewal
issue_body:
Suppose you originally obtained a certificate using the following command:

```
letsencrypt certonly --webroot --webroot-map '{"example.com,example.org" : "/var/www/html"}'
```

When you to renew the certificate, suppose the webroot paths have changed so you use the command:

```
letsencrypt renew --webroot-map '{"example.com" : "/var/www/com", "example.org" : "/var/www/org"}'
```

When you do this, you get the following error message:

> Error: Currently, the renew verb is only capable of renewing all installed certificates that are due to be renewed; individual domains cannot be specified with this action. If you would like to renew specific certificates, use the certonly command. The renew verb may provide other options for selecting certificates to renew in the future.

This problem is caused because `--webroot-map` modifies domains which currently isn't allowed to be modified during renewal.

issue_title: GetParticipantsRequest  gets only 10000 Members and not more.
issue_body:
Python 3.6

I am working on a project to get a list of members of a group or channel:

I followed the code sample given in the doc to retrieve all the members:

[Click Here](http://telethon.readthedocs.io/en/latest/extra/examples/chats-and-channels.html#retrieving-all-chat-members-channels-too)

http://telethon.readthedocs.io/en/latest/extra/examples/chats-and-channels.html#retrieving-all-chat-members-channels-too

```
offset = 0
limit = 100
all_participants = []
channel = 'thecoinfarm'
while True:
    participants = client(GetParticipantsRequest(
        channel, ChannelParticipantsRecent(), offset, limit,
        hash=0
    ))
    if not participants.users:
        break
    all_participants.extend(participants.users)
    offset += len(participants.users)
    print(offset)
```

I saw that it can only retrieve 10000  members and not more. The given test channel has approx 27 thousand members and I was able to extract only 10000 of them, so my question is, is that the limitation from telegram?

I went through the telegram API doc and was not able to find this limitation, I also tried with a negative offset in an attempt to traverse from the back but it didn't work.

Just wanted to know if it is possible to get all of the member lists.
Thanks for creating this project though.

issue_title: Remove hook set by past.autotranslate
issue_body:
In [__init__.py](https://github.com/common-workflow-language/schema_salad/blob/master/schema_salad/__init__.py#L32) the past.autotranslate function is used. This function hooks the Py2Fixer loader to sys.meta_path.  This is an unwanted side effect and can result in errors when importing schema_salad in python 3 code (see [this issue](https://github.com/nlppln/nlppln/issues/27))

A quick fix would be to remove the hook after  the autotranslate:
```python
from past.translation import remove_hooks
remove_hooks()
```

issue_title: Powerline/zsh extremely slow in git repos with large file counts
issue_body:
Hey,

I'm finding zsh with powerline takes a long time to finish commands such as `ls`, `cd` in git repos with high file counts.. Is this a known issue? 

For example, the following project:

https://github.com/shakacode/react-webpack-rails-tutorial

Once npm install is run on this, the directory and its subdirectories will contain around 32000 files. The vast majority are `.gitignore`d, but the presence of so many files in a git repo seems to be the deciding factor for the massive slow downs I'm seeing: Powerline/zsh are really speedy in the root directory, and in repos with a low file count. In rbenv's gem installation paths, eg `$HOME/.rbenv/versions/2.1.2/lib/ruby/gems/2.1.0/gems/railties-4.2.2`, or just generally under `$/.rbenv` (190~k files) things slow down heavily, but in a normal rails project everything is fine.

In the above project, the `client/node_modules` directory is the main offender, containing 29000 of the 32000 files. Note however that the problem is still exhibited in subdirectories outside of `node_modules`, eg `:/app/models`.

issue_title: Record errors for invalid types passed to Schema.load()
issue_body:
With `2.13.5`:

```python
In [1]: from marshmallow import Schema, fields

In [2]: Schema().load({})
Out[2]: UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={})

In [3]: Schema().load([])
Out[3]: UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={})

In [4]: Schema().load(123)
Out[4]: UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={})
```

1) I assume this doesn't produce errors, because there are no fields in the schema. However, as schema itself kinda represents `dict` type (=requires dict to parse values from), would it make sense to record error for `[]` and `123` etc.?

Then with `many=True`:

```
In [5]: Schema(many=True).load({})
Out[5]: UnmarshalResult(data=[], errors={})

In [6]: Schema(many=True).load([])
Out[6]: UnmarshalResult(data=[], errors={})

In [7]: Schema(many=True).load(123)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
TypeError                                 Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-7-8ad8cdeda05d> in <module>()
----> 1 Schema(many=True).load(123)

/home/musttu/Code/virtualenvs/pastry/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.pyc in load(self, data, many, partial)
    578         .. versionadded:: 1.0.0
    579         """
--> 580         result, errors = self._do_load(data, many, partial=partial, postprocess=True)
    581         return UnmarshalResult(data=result, errors=errors)
    582 

/home/musttu/Code/virtualenvs/pastry/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.pyc in _do_load(self, data, many, partial, postprocess)
    658                     partial=partial,
    659                     dict_class=self.dict_class,
--> 660                     index_errors=self.opts.index_errors,
    661                 )
    662             except ValidationError as error:

/home/musttu/Code/virtualenvs/pastry/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/marshalling.pyc in deserialize(self, data, fields_dict, many, partial, dict_class, index_errors, index)
    237                         partial=partial, dict_class=dict_class,
    238                         index=idx, index_errors=index_errors)
--> 239                     for idx, d in enumerate(data)]
    240 
    241             self._pending = False

TypeError: 'int' object is not iterable
```

2) I would kinda expect `{}` to record error (because it's not list). And int value (non-iterable value) should be gracefully caught into errors, rather than throwing error?

If we add a sole field:

```
In [8]: class Foo(Schema):
   ...:     bar = fields.Str()
   ...:
```

When I use `ModelSchema`, and set `ordered=True` in class Meta, the output isn't the same order as my schema fields:

Demo:

    import marshmallow_sqlalchemy as mas
    class GearReviewSchema(mas.ModelSchema): 
          class Meta:
              ordered = True

          # schema fields... 

    gear_review_schema = GearReviewSchema()

    review = db.session.query(GearReview).get(3)

    gear_review_schema.dump(review)

    class GearReviewSchema(ma.Schema):

issue_title: Generated schemas do not have fields set with current Marshmallow release
issue_body:
Installing marshmallow@1.2.6 results in schemas generated via `ModelSchema` not having their fields set, thus dumping empty dicts. It looks like `get_declared_fields` was added in marshmallow-code/marshmallow@f993b891dffd20c33a6e4c089185c16465c6fcba, which is currently in the beta build. This commit fixes the issue.

Just a heads-up while we wait for the next marshmallow release. :P

issue_title: Use the SymEngine's C API instead of the C++
issue_body:
This has the following advantages:

1. Cython only needs to be run in the C mode, so it will be much faster to compile (i.e. gcc instead of g++), and we avoid the Cython bug #66.

2. The C wrappers will be much better tested and complete, since if we want to expose something to Python, we would have to wrap it in C, and thus Ruby/Julia and other languages can easily take advantage of this as well (and vice versa).

3. I think the Cython code will simplify quite a bit (no template tricks, etc.)

Possible disadvantage:

4. The result might possibly be slower than calling C++ directly in some cases. If that happens, we should think hard if there is a way to create a C wrapper with minimal overhead (I think we are already pretty good about this).

issue_title: Deprecation notices
issue_body:
In the C API, some functions are marked as deprecated:

* `Z3_mk_config`
* `Z3_del_config`
* `Z3_set_param_value`
* `Z3_mk_context`
* `Z3_update_param_value`

Notably, `Z3_mk_context_rc` is not deprecated.

But there's no way to create a `Z3_context` without creating a `Z3_config` object that I've seen.

The docs don't make it clear what the successor to these functions might be in all cases.

It might help to use compiler attributes to mark these functions as deprecated rather than only having it in the documentation comments as well?

I'm happy to submit a pull request for any of the above things, I just need to know what is best to do.

issue_title: Incorrect Glorot and He Weight Initialization of Transposed Convolution / Deconvolution Layers
issue_body:
Hi,

I think I found a bug in the weight initialization of TransposedConv2DLayer when Glorot or He initialization is used. Quick example to demonstrate:

```
import numpy as np
import theano.tensor as T
import lasagne

x = T.ftensor4()
l = lasagne.layers.InputLayer((None, 1, 16, 16), input_var=x)
l = lasagne.layers.TransposedConv2DLayer(l, 256, 1, 1, W=lasagne.init.HeUniform())

# you expect the range to be +- sqrt(3.0) * sqrt(1.0 / fan_in) where fan_in = 1 * 1 * 1
np.sqrt(3.0)
# = 1.73205...

# actual range is computed incorrectly because the number of output channels is used to compute fan_in
np.max(l.W.get_value())
# = 0.10825...
np.sqrt(3.0) * np.sqrt(1.0 / 256.0)
# = 0.10825...

```

The problem is that the He and Glorot initializers assume that index 1 of the weight matrix contains the number of channels in but for transposed convolutions it is reversed.
(Relevant code: [https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/init.py#L250-L251](https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/blob/master/lasagne/init.py#L250-L251))

issue_title: Kernel hooks to check output robustly
issue_body:
This is an ambitious idea, and I'm not yet entirely sure of the details.

Checking output data is often tricky: small changes can be meaningful, while large changes can easily be unimportant (e.g. in a PNG plot). It would be nice to have some hooks to communicate with kernels for more intelligent checks (e.g. using [plotchecker](https://github.com/jhamrick/plotchecker) on matplotlib plots).

How might this work? Could we define a special output mimetype which kernels would produce (e.g. `application/x-nbval-output-data`), and a way for the test framework to call a check function in the kernel like `check_output('saved output data', 'computed output data')`?

issue_title: scandir and container_files
issue_body:
Hi,

this might be the wrong place for this discussion. In case please suggest a more appropriate one.

We have some special requirements which we plan to build on top of pyfs2, and we hope you have some good ideas on how to achieve / implement this.
- We only need to read the data
- A scandir like function that **transparently** treats container files (*.tar.gz; *.zip) very much like a directory
- Whatever the iterator returns must include the full "virtual" path, e.g. /xxx/data.tar.gz/subdir/myfile and the fd related to the file (please see below for why that is)
- Some of our tar.gz files are (very) large and we'd like to use streaming (mode="r|*"). Based on previous experience this is significantly faster for our use case and setup. Streaming however comes with a caveat: seek()/tell() is not allowed and thus the iteration order is the order in which the files were added to the container file. That limitation is fine for us. 
- Due to the streaming requirement the my_scandir iterator needs to return a tuple of fd and info, rather then the filename. Using the filename to open requires seek/tell.
- This fd + info approach raises some issues with nested container files, because pyfs2 fs.open(path) does not support fd/fileobj, but only path. Python Tarfile does support tarfile.open(name=.., fileobj=...). This is required for nested container files only (unfortunately we are not able to change that).
- To my understanding streaming is currently only supported in python for tar-files , not for zip files. Which is fine for us. Where not supported, silently apply fd = fs.open(path).
- It is fine for us if the fd is a binary stream. We apply TextIOWrapper at a later stage where necessary

Do you have some good ideas on how to easily achieve this?

thanks a lot
Juergen

issue_title: Dependency tree not propagating resolution
issue_body:
#### Code Sample, a minimal, complete, and verifiable piece of code

```python
from datetime import datetime

from satpy.scene import Scene
from satpy.utils import debug_on
import glob

debug_on()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    scn = Scene(
        sensor="viirs",
        start_time=datetime(2015, 3, 11, 11, 15),
        end_time=datetime(2015, 3, 11, 11, 30),
        filenames=glob.glob("/home/a001673/data/satellite/Suomi-NPP/viirs/lvl1b/2015/03/11/SDR/*"),

        reader="viirs_sdr")
    composite = 'true_color_lowres'
    scn.load([composite])
```

Add a breakpoint at the beginning of the `Scene.compute` method, and print out the dependency tree:
```python
pdb> print(self.dep_tree)
```

#### Problem description

Currently, the dependency search for composites doesn't propagate the correct resolution, leading to unnecessary complication of the composite and modifier configs in the yaml files.

For example, such a yaml definition:

```yaml
  rayleigh_corrected:
    compositor: !!python/name:satpy.composites.PSPRayleighReflectance
    atmosphere: us-standard
    aerosol_type: marine_clean_aerosol
    prerequisites:
    - name: M05
      modifiers: [sunz_corrected]
    optional_prerequisites:
    - satellite_azimuth_angle
    - satellite_zenith_angle
    - solar_azimuth_angle
    - solar_zenith_angle

  true_color_lowres:
    compositor: !!python/name:satpy.composites.RGBCompositor
    prerequisites:
    - name: M05
      modifiers: [sunz_corrected, rayleigh_corrected]
    - name: M04
      modifiers: [sunz_corrected, rayleigh_corrected]
    - name: M03
      modifiers: [sunz_corrected, rayleigh_corrected]
    standard_name: true_color
```
where the angles are defined in both M- and I-band resolution leads to the wrong resolution being chosen under certain circumstances (for example when the I-band angles are defined first in the yaml dataset list).

If no modification is made to the above yaml, the presented code crashes down the line because of not having access to the M-band resolution angles.

#### Expected Behaviour

I'm expecting the dependency resolution to choose the right resolution when multiple resolutions are available for some datasets but not others.

Of course, creating per-resolution modifiers fixes the problem, but I find this ugly and non-optimal.

issue_title: Support for Devuan Linux
issue_body:
Currently you cannot install the nixstatsagent on a Devuan Linux machine using the wget commands provided by nixstats.com's "Add Server" button. It will throw an error with a list of supported distros.

Devuan Linux ([devuan.org](https://devuan.org/)) is a fork of Debian that aims to act as an replacement for people who do not agree with Debian's inclusion of systemd.

If possible, I believe Devuan support should be added because:
1. It is a distro garnering increasing amounts of attention (Being ranked 36 on Distrowatch right now)
2. As of now it is mostly identical to Debian except for the init system, so it should be relatively easy to add support

issue_title: New LHCb Gaudi application type
issue_body:
A new LHCb application type should be created. It should work with the new structure for the private relase areas based on lb-dev and lb-run.

## Connection to old Gaudi types
There should be no connection to the old application types for Gaudi based applications.

## New GaudiRun application type
* The new application type should be called *GaudiRun* (as a way to distinguish it from the existing 
*GaudiPython* type). It will not need any specialisation for Brunel/DaVinci etc.
* The schema will have
  *  is_prepared = None ,
  * extraopts = None ,
  *  args = [-T]  ,
  *  platform = 'x86_64-slc6-gcc48-opt' ,
   * lb-runtOptions = '' ,
  *  location = '' ,
  *  optsfile = []  
 The *location* attribute is where the release is located. There default value should now be empty for this and the job submission should fail if empty.

## Implementation
 * Marco Clemencic will provide script that can encapsulate environment.
 * Marco Clemencic will provide build target which can pack up local shared libraries.
 * Caching of the environment for running should initially be avoided as there are some tricky issues
 * Implementation that works without local shared libraries should be possible initially without the
  build target above in place.

issue_title: exportToGPI test bug
issue_body:
As part of #192 I've discovered a few problems with the startup/stop ganga methods.

Basically I've discovered the problem that exported 'Objects' in the GPI should be replaced during a new instance during the ganga startup, but unfortunately they are not.


The result of this is that tests are inconsistently addressing objects which in some cases should have been destroyed (or attempted to be destroyed between tests by the shutdown system).

This only effects objects exposed via the GPI as we expect functions and classes to remain the same.
However, the objects exposed this way includes the Repositories as well as the queues system.

I'm working on a fix for 6.1.18 (or later) which makes sure that on a fresh ganga startup these objects within the GPI are replaced with a new instance.

This directly effects the bug that we saw that the repository directory doesn't follow changes in the 'gangadir' between tests so would allow us to move toward making sure that each test is run on it's own clean repository in the future.

issue_title: Update test selection for pulp_python 2.0
issue_body:
I'm not entirely sure, but I believe that we test the pulp_python plugin as though it were version 2.0 if the platform version is 2.12.2 or greater. There was a bit of confusion around exactly what version of Pulp would ship with pulp_python 2.0 that was resolved recently. Now, we're going to be releasing pulp_python 2.0 with Pulp 2.13.0.

I think this means that selectors [like this](https://github.com/PulpQE/pulp-smash/blob/master/pulp_smash/tests/python/api_v2/test_sync_publish.py#L115) (where both a bug is checked for testability and a version is checked) should be updated to not be tested unless the Pulp under test is 2.13 or greater. The bugs related to these new python features will be returning to a testable state shortly and will likely cause false negative results in runs on 2.12.z, but should succeed on builds for Pulp 2.13:

- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/135
- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/136
- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/140
- https://pulp.plan.io/issues/1882

issue_title: Can't use custom field as input in mutation
issue_body:
I have a custom field (written with @syrusakbary help in [this issue](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene/issues/433)):

```python
from functools import partial

class ProtoEnum(graphene.Field):
    def __init__(self, protobuf_enum, *args, **kwargs):
        super(ProtoEnum, self).__init__(graphene.Int, *args, **kwargs)
        self.protobuf_enum = protobuf_enum

    def get_resolver(self, parent_resolver):
        return partial(_enum_resolver, self.protobuf_enum, parent_resolver)

def _enum_resolver(protobuf_enum, parent_resolver, root, args, context, info):
    resolved_value = parent_resolver(root, args, context, info)
    if resolved_value not in protobuf_enum.values():
        raise ValueError('Enum value "%s" is invalid' % resolved_value)
    return resolved_value
```

I wanted to use it as a input in my mutation like so:

```python
class Input:
    my_field = ProtoEnum(...)
```
Which gave me this error:

```
File "third_party/python3/graphene/types/field.py", line 54, in __init__
  File "third_party/python3/graphene/types/argument.py", line 61, in to_arguments
ValueError: Expected my_field to be Argument, but received ProtoEnum. Try using Argument(Int!).
```

I tried wrapping it in `Argument`, which in turn gave me this error:

```

AttributeError: 'ProtoEnum' object has no attribute '_meta'
```

I tried adding a `Meta` class but that didn't do anything. I can use custom field just fine in my schema otherwise.

Am I doing something wrong or is this just not supported?

issue_title: Automatically generating resolve functions
issue_body:
At the bottom of the official tutorial it says

>As you can see, GraphQL is very powerful but there are a lot of repetitions in our example. We can do a lot of improvements by adding layers of abstraction on top of `graphene-django`.

However, in the 1.0 blog post it says

>Ok, it’s hard to do a maintainable Python package based in the in the syntactic sugar magic behind the metaclasses.

I'm new to Python metaclasses and thought it would be a cool challenge to try and automatically add resolve functions for certain fields. I basically want to make `resolve_*` functions be automatically added in simple cases:

```
class Query(graphene.AbstractType):
    lesson = graphene.Field(LessonType, id=graphene.Int())

    # def resolve_lesson(self, args, context, info):
    #     This will get automatically added!
    #     id = args.get('id')

    #     if slug is not None:
    #         return Lesson.objects.get(id=id)

    #     return None
```

I tried to do this by defining my own metaclass inheriting from `AbstractTypeMeta`, and adding extra resolve functions to attrs before `AbstractTypeMeta` does its thing. Unfortunately due to the way the schemas are combined with `graphene.ObjectType`, I get a metaclass conflict. At this point I gave up as it seems like I'm either missing something or it will be a long headache if I continue.

My question is, are there any capabilities or plans to be able to do what I want to do?

By the way, very excited to start using GraphQL! I've only just discovered it today but it looks very promising.

issue_title: Diagnostic commands
issue_body:
Currently OpenThread supports some of diagnostic commands (DIAG_GET.req, DIAG_GET.rsp and DIAG_RST.nrf). CLI example exposes command `networkdiagnostic get`, however a response is not populated back (see `NetworkDiagnostic::HandleDiagnosticGetResponse`).

I would like to get those information on CLI at least in raw tlv ASCII format e.g.: 
`DIAG_GET.ans: 0102fc00`
So the Openthread public API callback could be like: `otSetDiagnosticGetCallback(otInstance, callback(otMessage))`

Other option could be to parse DIAG_GET.rsp in the `NetworkDiagnostic::HandleDiagnosticGetResponse` and return some kind of structure (but who should allocate the memory for it, since DIAG_GET.ans consists of different number of members e.g. could carry 4 IPv6 addresses and 3 child information etc.) - however it may be challenging taking to account the amount of possible Diagnostic TLVs.

So generally there are two questions:
1. How do you want to Diagnostic Get callback to look like in Openthread API (return only otMessage or parsed structure etc.)
2. How to show responses on the CLI? Is it acceptable to print only raw payload in ASCII?

Also as a side task it might be usuful to implement two left commands DIAG_GET.qry and DIAG_GET.ans which are almost the same as already implemented with a difference that they are better for multicast queries due to different transaction pattern.

issue_title: Requesting new Network Data if Child Update Response contains new version
issue_body:
Right now when a child handles Child Update Response message it calls `HandleLeaderData()`. Child Update Response does not contain Active or Pending Timestamps and because of that `HandleLeaderData()` does not send Data Request packet even if Network Data version in the message is greater than local value. It seems that Data Request should be sent in this case.

It is not a big problem because there is fallback procedure to check Network Data version in Advertisements transmitted by parent and this procedure works as expected.

issue_title: Dask wrappers/interface for pyFFTW
issue_body:
Often I use FFTs on large data and am eager to get any speed up I can with it. pyFFTW has been great at delivering really nice performance while also making it very easy to use FFTW thanks to the NumPy style interface.

Though one thing that would be really great is to make it easier still to leverage pyFFTW on data much larger than memory and handle this in parallel. One library that is really fantastic for this is Dask. By allowing one to treat massive files like NumPy arrays and leverage delayed computation, one is able to construct different workflows with large data while keeping focusing on the big picture.

In an attempt to get these two great libraries to play together, @mrocklin, @jcrist, and I worked together on abstracting an FFT interface in Dask ( https://github.com/dask/dask/pull/2093 ) to allow one to easily wrap FFT functions from external libraries that match the NumPy API. While doing this I made sure to test against pyFFTW explicitly. It is trivial for any user to roll these themselves. However, I wondered if it would make sense to included some prewrapped copies of these functions in pyFFTW as part of a new Dask interface for instance.

Would be curious to know your thoughts on this proposal.

issue_title: Renewing certificates documentation is ambiguous regarding hooks persistence
issue_body:
After certbot automatically renewed its certificates, I realized I would need to restart services to read the new certs.

The user's guide section on renewing certificates (https://certbot.eff.org/docs/using.html#renewing-certificates) says that I can use `certbot renew --renew-hook /path/to/renew-hook-script` to specify a script that will restart services.

I am confused, however, about what behaviour this would result in next time certificates are automatically renewed.  If I run `certbot renew --renew-hook my-script` once, will it run `my-script` on automatic renewal?  If not, how do I specify that the hook should be executed on automatic renewal?  If so, how would I see which scripts would be run on automatic renewal?

The confusion is exacerbated because there are multiple places on the system where this information could reside: a crontab that invokes certbot renew, systemd's certbot.service, or in certbot's configuration in /etc/.

For someone who has been running certbot, the user guide section is the natural place to look for answers to the questions above, so my feature request is to have these questions clarified in that section of the documentation.

issue_title: SSL_DHPARAMS_SRC captures buildpath in docs
issue_body:
Hello certbot-krew!

Because constants.py is evaluated at build time in order to generate documentation, the `pkg_resources.resource_filename` path is evaluated to include the build path.  At run time, the correct value is used from where it is installed to, but the build path being captured inside the documentation makes the package not binary reproducible.  (https://tests.reproducible-builds.org/debian/rb-pkg/unstable/amd64/python-certbot.html)

That seems like the only constant that's being dynamically generated like that; would it be possible to exclude it from the documentation?

Thanks!

server {
    listen MYIP:443 ssl http2;
    root /var/www/;
    server_name www.mydomain.com ; 
    index index.php index.html index.htm;
    access_log off;
    error_log /var/log/nginx/error.log crit;

    error_page 404 /404.html;

    include /etc/nginx/include/404;
    
    if (!-d $request_filename) {
        rewrite ^/(.*)/$ /$1 permanent;
    }    


    location ~ "^/([a-zA-Z]{2})$" {
        rewrite "^/([a-zA-Z]{2})$" /inc/mycountry.php?country=$1 last;
    }      

    location ~* \.(?:ico|css|js|gif|jpe?g|png)$ {
        expires 30d;
        add_header Pragma public;
        add_header Cache-Control "public";
    }    


    location /news {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /news/index.php?$args;
    }
    location /feedback {
        rewrite ^/feedback$ /inc/myfeedback.php last;
    }    

    location ~* /store/ {
        rewrite ^/.*/store/([0-9]+)- /inc/mystore.php?store=$1 last;
    }    


    location /product/ {
        #rewrite ^/product/([0-9]+)-.*/([0-9]+)-.* /inc/myproduct.php?id_device=$1&id_sub_device=$2 last;
        #rewrite ^/product/([0-9]+)-.* /inc/myproduct.php?id_device=$1 last;
        rewrite ^/product/(.*)/(.*) /inc/myproduct.php?device=$1&sub_device=$2 last;
        rewrite ^/product/(.*) /inc/myproduct.php?device=$1 last;
    }    

    location = / {
        rewrite ^/$ /inc/myhome.php last;
    }    
    
    location = /browser {
        rewrite ^/browser$ /inc/mybrowser.php last;
    }   
    location = /privacy {
        rewrite ^/privacy$ /inc/myprivacy.php last;
    }   
    location = /about {
        rewrite ^/about$ /inc/myprivacy.php last;
    }              

    location ~ /\.git {
      return 404;
      #deny all;
    }

    include /etc/nginx/include/php;
    include /etc/nginx/include/ssl;
}

issue_title: Adding RPi GPIO switch functionality
issue_body:
Hello,

I don´t know if this is the right place to post a question like this, but I´ll try.

I´m new to Python and programming in general, but I´m learning.

Currently I´m working on a project with a standalone GCS unit based on two Raspberry Pi´s in a Peli case together with two 7" Raspberry touchscreens. One for the telemetry and one for a video downlink. (I´m using a RPi with Navio2 as flightcontroller).

I´ve successfully got the "telemetry" RPi with a custom USB joystick interface up and running with MavProxy and communicating with my Navio2. Now I´m studying the MavProxy code to find a way to issue some predefined terminal commands like "arm throttle" "rtl" etc with switches connected to the RPi GPIO or with my USB joystick interface by mapping the buttons to trigger a standalone script to do the tasks I want. 

Basically I want to trigger the mavproxy_arm.py and mavproxy_mode.py with switches connected to the RPi.

I´m stuck. Can anyone point me in the right direction?  

Best regards
Alex from Norway

issue_title: Config options allowed_groups and search_filter are mutually exclusive
issue_body:
As per the title, these two options cannot be used together.

If the bind operation was successful, there is an if (groups) else if (filter):
https://github.com/jupyterhub/ldapauthenticator/blob/630c512f6fea871be137cf902ce5d0f20ceec0c7/ldapauthenticator/ldapauthenticator.py#L338
https://github.com/jupyterhub/ldapauthenticator/blob/630c512f6fea871be137cf902ce5d0f20ceec0c7/ldapauthenticator/ldapauthenticator.py#L359

In particular, this means that for our case, where we don't actually bind with a DN, but with user@domain (this works with AD and we use it to avoid the first bind with the "technical account"), we can't restrict for group membership, because we need to FIRST do the search for the actual DN, using the filter.

You could argue that we should use the DN lookup feature, but that requires some static account (you call it the "technical account" but we neither have, nor want this), as it is not possible to use the given username/password for the first bind operation in resolve_username. (This could be opened as a different issue or feature request).
https://github.com/jupyterhub/ldapauthenticator/blob/630c512f6fea871be137cf902ce5d0f20ceec0c7/ldapauthenticator/ldapauthenticator.py#L227


As a side note not aimed specifically at you guys, I think it is generally unfortunate that every project reinvents the wheel wrt. the configuration of LDAP-based authentication modules. Even if you happen to know how you can bind and what you need to search for (and that might be a capital if), you end up fiddling with options and reading docs / source code until you figure out a config that works with project X.

Regards,
M.

issue_title: Does nfcpy always polls when waiting for a NFC tag?
issue_body:
We use the following code to wait for a NFC tag but we are unsure on how nfcpy handles this underneath...

```
import nfc

def connected(tag):
    """ Print the NFC Tag information"""

clf = nfc.ContactlessFrontend('tty:AMA0:pn532')

timeOut = lambda: time.time() - started > (5 * 60) 
started = time.time()
nfcTag = clf.connect(rdwr={'on-connect': connected}, terminate=timeOut)

""" Do something with the tag here"""
```

We are concerned that nfcpy always poll waiting for a tag, thus the power consumption on our device is not optional.

Any feedback will be really appreciated...

issue_title: Provide a way for tf.Print to work in Datalab
issue_body:
Currently tf.Print prints stuff in c stdout, which is not reflected in sys.stdout. Users have to apply workarounds like http://stackoverflow.com/questions/37898478/is-there-a-way-to-get-tensorflow-tf-print-output-to-appear-in-jupyter-notebook-o. to get tf.Print stuff showing up. 

One proposal is for Datalab to provide a context, such that tf.Print works if we write code like:

import tensorflow as tf
import google.datalab.capture_stdout as cs:

with cs.Capture():
  with tf.Graph().as_default():
    # Create a few example ops
    a = tf.constant(1)
    a = tf.Print(a, [a], "value of a")
    # Example ops end

    with tf.Session() as sess:
      sess.run(tf.Print(a, [a]))

issue_title: Bulk upsert example incorrect
issue_body:
In the Readme, the bulk upsert data example is as follows:

```python
data = [
  {'Id': '0000000000AAAAA', 'Email': 'examplenew2@example.com'},
  {'Id': '', 'Email': 'foo@foo.com'}
]
```
based on my testing and usage of this query, dictionaries that had `'Id': ''` were not actually inserted. Only by removing the `Id` key was I able to get it to work. 

I'm happy to make the PR to update this unless there's something I'm missing.

issue_title: Support multiple schemas validations
issue_body:
Currently `beetmoverscript` and `shipitscript` support more than one behavior (where a behavior is usually defined by a separate `action` type), hence multiple schemas validation to take care of. For all these cases, we can't take advantage of scriptworker's implicit validation, at least not in an elegant way.

Beetmover was the first one with multiple behaviors and frankly, is still a mess from this perspective (`nightly` and `release` schemas where former is almost inexisting in unittesting, etc). Now, with adding multiple behaviors in `shipitscript`, it might be handy to enable that in order to reduce the boilerplate code we need.

Ah, just reminded, I think we have multiple behaviors in `bouncerscript` as well (submission + aliases). I've been doing mostly tiny reviews in this project so far so I'd love if I could take care of implementing this in order to get even more confortable with the code.

issue_title: Add transaction_hash to ContractReceiveStateChange
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Currently the `ContractReceiveStateChange` includes the `transaction_from` which was supposed to be used to update the transaction queue when replaying the WAL. It isn't used for this any more and should be replaced instead with the transaction hash, so that it is available for querying additional infos when upgrading the WAL.

issue_title: Retry registration in case of insufficient funds
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Selecting an account that doesn't hold enough ether to perform the registration transaction causes an exception (including traceback) to be printed to the console. The raiden node continues to run but of course isn't registered at this point.

## Solution

The user should be prompted to fund the account and a retry logic should be implemented.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Implement registration retry
- [ ] Implement message prompting user to fund the account

issue_title: Inline formset fields have 'required' attribute (Django 1.10 issue)
issue_body:
When using ``polymorphic_inlineformset_factory()`` it seems that the resulting forms have the ``required`` HTML attribute set, even though they should actually be optional.

I tracked this as far as the ``Form.use_required_attribute``, [which was added in Django 1.10](https://github.com/django/django/commit/ec6121693f112ae33b653b4364e812722d2eb567). This is now set in Django's own formset code, but isn't set by django-polymorphic.

However, I couldn't quite figure out how to actually fix this. I also only have so much time available so unfortunately I need to move on. I hope that this will help someone find a solution.

issue_title: Using an alternative kernel or language for pre-defined subkernel
issue_body:
Currently
```
%use R -l myR
```
is disallowed because `R` is pre-defined by SoS to work with local `ir` kernel and language `R`. 

However, for example, if we

1. Create a notebook with local `ir`
2. Move to another machine and would like to connect to the original `ir` kernel (on the remote machine)  because it has the modules we need and/or exact versions of modules if we would like to reproduce the analysis.

We can do

```
%use R-remote -k R-remote
```
then we would have to change the `R` kernel to `R-remote` for all cells.

It would be easier to allow

```
%use R -k R-remote
```

@jirikuncar : is this scenario reasonable?

issue_title: Change parameters to be type-specific
issue_body:
I've been using parameters a lot as object attributes, both for instrument drivers, and also for objects that are completely unrelated to physical instruments (meta-instruments).
Doing so has some clear advantages:
- Integration with QCoDeS loops (makes them sweepable and measurable),
- Allows a standard way to add attribute metadata (e.g. docstring, labels, validators, etc.),
- Integration with extra tools, such as a monitor.

However, one of the things that I've struggled with is when exactly to use a parameter, and when to use straightforward object attributes (`self.attr = some_value`). This becomes especially relevant for meta-instruments, as there not every parameter corresponds to an instrument instruction. The root of the problem seems to be that parameters don't really behave the same way as attributes. For example, getting/setting a parameter is done by calling it (`param(new_val)`), instead of simply assigning a value. (`param(new_val)` instead of `param = new_val`). This leads to the annoying situation where I have to think for every attribute if it actually is a parameter or not. 

Ideally, we would want parameters to behave more like attributes, in which case we wouldn't have to worry about if an attribute is actually a parameter or not. any attribute relevant for QCoDeS could be a parameter (or even all attributes, although this might cause unnecessary overhead). I've been thinking about a way to tackle this problem, and my proposed solution is to have type-specific descriptor parameters. As an example, say we have a ListParameter:

```python
class ListParameter(list, Parameter)
    def __get__(self, instance, owner):
        return self
    def __set__(self, instance, val):
        self(val)

list_parameter = ListParameter()
```

By having the ListParameter inherit from both `list` and `Parameter`, we allow it to behave as a list, and get the additional functionality of a parameter. We can therefore treat it exactly like a list (e.g. `list_parameter.append(val)`, while it also has all the Parameter features (e.g. `list_parameter.unit`).

Furthermore, if we attach the ListParameter to an instrument (`instr.list_parameter`), the descriptor methods (`__get__` and `__set__`) allow simple getting/setting of values (e.g. `instr_list_parameter = new_val`). Note that this only works when the ListParameter is an attribute of another object, otherwise we would still need to use `list_parameter(new_val)`. This could also solve a few other issues. A MultiParameter would simply be a ListParameter, validators would usually be implicit, but could still be overridden for additional conditions such as being a multiple of a value. 

One of the main issues with these type-specific parameters is dealing with immutable types, such as `int`. In this case, we cannot change its value once it's instantiated, and so we need another solution for this. The only solution I have come up with so far is to have the IntParameter mimick the int class, but being mutable. However, I can imagine there could be a better solution, so if someone has one please come forward :)

Have a look at this line:
https://github.com/fastai/fastai/blob/13a8b6247c6278e1a56d5790beafcd6592639301/fastai/transforms.py#L173

You assume that method `__call__` can be potentially accessed from multiple threads, hence you added the RLock there.  But as soon as set_state finishes the thread go to next line and the lock is freed, leaving the state open for further changes. Unfortunately the next line:
https://github.com/fastai/fastai/blob/13a8b6247c6278e1a56d5790beafcd6592639301/fastai/transforms.py#L175

Assumes that the set_state stays unchanged during the whole execution, this is how RandomCrop is able to Crop both x and y in the same way. Unfortunately, the state can be changed since the `self.lock` was released.

To fix that  we should guard the whole execution like this:
```
 with self.lock:
      self.set_state()
      x,y = ((self.transform(x),y) if self.tfm_y==TfmType.NO
              else self.transform(x,y) if self.tfm_y==TfmType.PIXEL
              else self.transform_coord(x,y)) 
```

But that if I'm not mistaken that will kill any benefits that we have from having the transformations run in parallel.

The best would be to either pass state as paramters or use thread local variables (https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1408171/thread-local-storage-in-python).

I can propose a fix but since there is no unit test I'm not sure if I won't break anything. I'm trying to use the TfmType.PIXEL to create UNet implementation for the DataBowl, so once I have this somehow fixed I will add tests and then create PR.

issue_title: [Discussion] Saving the field object
issue_body:
**Usage:**
The field object is critical to checkpointing as it provides:

- tokenization
- padding
- numericalize

Having the ability to save the field object allows the user, given arbitrary text, to preprocess the text. The preprocessed text is then used with a checkpointed model. Then the output is predicted and interpreted without the output dictionary.

**Problem:**
`torch.save` is implemented with pickle. The field object accepts lambdas for tokenization, preprocessing and postprocessing; therefore, cannot be pickled. 

**Key Points:**
- The vocab object needs to be pickled because the output of the model is uninterpretable without it.

**Discussion:**
What is the right abstraction here?
Should the vocab object be saved and the field object discarded?
Is it appropriate to have the field object and the vocab object closely bound?

issue_title: `new <url>` and `up <url>` should work with Gists
issue_body:
Idea from Joe. Opening this issue to track the scenario.

This way we can do things like use Gists for complete programs in blog posts and be able to mention `pulumi new` or `pulumi up` gestures inline (or even embed the "Deploy with Pulumi" button) to deploy a working example straight off the blog.

Gists are Git repos so this should already mostly just-work. We should consider adding some special handling to allow regular Gist URLs to be passed (that we massage into a "cloneable URL" automatically).

### Source code / logs
#### Code
```
"""Example of a custom gym environment. Run this for a demo."""

from __future__ import absolute_import
from __future__ import division
from __future__ import print_function

import numpy as np
import gym
from gym.spaces import Discrete, Box
from gym.envs.registration import EnvSpec

import ray
from ray.tune import run_experiments
from ray.tune.registry import register_env


class SimpleCorridor(gym.Env):
    """Example of a custom env in which you have to walk down a corridor.
    You can configure the length of the corridor via the env config."""

    def __init__(self, config):
        self.end_pos = config["corridor_length"]
        self.cur_pos = 0
        self.action_space = Discrete(2)
        self.observation_space = Box(
            0.0, self.end_pos, shape=(1,), dtype=np.float32)
        self._spec = EnvSpec("SimpleCorridor-{}-v0".format(self.end_pos))

    def reset(self):
        self.cur_pos = 0
        return [self.cur_pos]

    def step(self, action):
        assert action in [0, 1]
        if action == 0 and self.cur_pos > 0:
            self.cur_pos -= 1
        elif action == 1:
            self.cur_pos += 1
        done = self.cur_pos >= self.end_pos
        return [self.cur_pos], 1 if done else 0, done, {}


if __name__ == "__main__":
    env_creator_name = "corridor"
    register_env(env_creator_name, lambda config: SimpleCorridor(config))
    ray.init()
    run_experiments({
        "demo": {
            "run": "PPO",
            "env": "corridor",
```

issue_title: [XRay] GPU label support
issue_body:
Start two raylets:

```
ray start --head --use-raylet --num-gpus 1
```

```
ray start --redis-address 172.31.23.99:28629 --use-raylet --num-gpus 1
```

Execute this script:

```
from __future__ import absolute_import
from __future__ import division
from __future__ import print_function

import ray

@ray.remote(num_gpus=1)
class Worker(object):

    def __init__(self):
        pass

    def get_node_ip(self):
        return ray.services.get_node_ip_address()

ray.init(redis_address="172.31.23.99:28629", use_raylet=True)
ws = [Worker.remote() for i in range(10)]

print("XXX", [ray.get(w.get_node_ip.remote()) for w in ws])
```

Output:
```
XXX ['172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99', '172.31.23.99']
```

issue_title: [rllib] Allow passing in additional TF operations for evaluation for PPO (e.g. for batch normalization)
issue_body: I would like to use a simple TF model with batch normalization along with PPO. I am considering the following TF feature - https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/layers/batch_normalization. The problem I am facing is that in order for the above batch normalization feature to work, you have to run the moving mean/variance update operations, in addition to the NN graph computation. As far as I can tell, there is no way of passing these operations into `extra_ops` in `self.par_opt.optimize(extra_ops=...)` call inside `PPOEvaluator.run_sgd_minibatch(..)`. Is anyone aware of a workaround that I could use? Is there perhaps a proper way of doing this that I have missed? Apologies if this is a dumb questions, I am fairly new to both TF and rllib.

issue_title: Enable certificate verification for Twitch
issue_body:
### Description

Certificate verification is disable when making API calls in the Twitch plugin. According to comments in the code, "The certificate used by Twitch cannot be verified on some OpenSSL versions." The commit to disable verification was done 3 years ago during Livestreamer development, but the specifics are unclear. 

It might be risky to re-enable it and wait for people to complain that it doesn't work. The issue could be limited to Python 2.6 or perhaps Windows? I have no problems enabling certificate verification on Python 2.7/3.5/3.6 under Linux or macOS.

The question is how should we go about re-enabling the verification?

### Expected / Actual behaviour

Streamlink should _always_ verify SSL certificates, but it does not.

### Environment details

Streamlink and Python version: `streamlink<=0.6.0`

Related to #963

This was indirectly requested in #150 .

issue_title: Should incomplete ties also get hashes?
Understanding one_span_per_rpc configuration
issue_body:
I just spent a while looking into problems with traces being improperly nested when dealing with calls between Go and Python services. It turned out that the needed fix was to set what appears to be an undocumented `one_span_per_rpc` option to false on my tracer, allowing a new span identifier to be created so that is is a proper child of the request calling my HTTP endpoint. This is the relevant code: https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python/blob/bbb07d46c03f0e4c369dbca93fda6011b3b39fae/jaeger_client/tracer.py#L147-L153

I see the documentation about supporting zipkin-style behavior, but am unclear on why that is the default. I'm using the jaeger, not zipkin, which I would expect is the primary use case for this library?

Is this something that it would make sense to better document and expose through the `Config` object?

issue_title: Check whether the enrichment produced the expected result
issue_body:
Due to the number of studies will increase during the next months, it is needed to design a way to allow the system to check whether the result obtained is the expected. The example of this is the "areas of code" analysis, where it is quite difficult to know whether the data set is correctly produced based on the raw data.

The idea of checking whether the result obtained is "correct" can be applied to more basic enrichment processes as Git where the relationship between raw and enriched is 1:1. 

From the operations point of view, we would have to allow our users to know whether the result written in the enridhed indexes is the one expected or if any error was found. This information could be both added to the log or even to a dedicated index so it could be displayed on the dashboard itself.

From my point of view, I would expect to have a design to do this for all data sources and studies and a basic implementation for them. Let's imagine an example of how this could be written to the log:
* for basic enrichments like Git: [quality-check] Git enrichment passed the checks
* for enrichments 1:n, n:n something like: [quality-check] Areas of code study passed the checks

issue_title: CasADi numeric equivalence check incomplete
issue_body:
The current check generates random input data like:

```Python
            for i in range(this.n_in()):
                sp = this.sparsity_in(0)
                r = ca.DM(sp, np.random.random(sp.nnz()))
                args_in.append(r)
```

```Python
This should probably be:
            for i in range(this.n_in()):
                sp = this.sparsity_in(i)  # CHANGED: i instead of 0
                r = ca.DM(sp, np.random.random(sp.nnz()))
                args_in.append(r)
```
Apparently CasADi automatically broadcasts a DM/float object to the correct shape, so this was not caught before. Making this change breaks some test cases (containing array expressions) however, which does not bode well.

issue_title: BearerToken estimate_type may be backwards
issue_body: The code which calls `estimate_type` for each token (in oauthlib.oauth2.rfc6749.endpoints.resource) sorts them by the return value of `estimate_type` using the built-in `sorted` function. However, `BearerToken` seems to return a high value to indicate that it should be used while `sorted` defaults to ascending. One of these seems backwards.

issue_title: Unable to install directly from repo
issue_body:
When trying to install `conu` directly from the this repo—using any of the below mentioned methods—the command fails throwing an error like the following —

_Installation methods_

- `$ pip3 install --user .`
- `$ python3 setup.py install`
- `$ pip3 install git+git://github.com/user-cont/conu.git`

_Error message_
```console
[sumit@hal9000 conu]$ pip3 install --user .
Processing /home/sumit/Coding/conu
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
      File "/tmp/pip-lf6poj06-build/setup.py", line 75, in <module>
        install_requires=get_requirements()
      File "/tmp/pip-lf6poj06-build/setup.py", line 35, in get_requirements
        with open("./requirements.txt") as fd:
    FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: './requirements.txt'
...
```

It's clear that it's happening because `setup.py` still contains a reference to a `requirements.txt`, but it was renamed to `requirements.in`. I'm not entirely sure why it was done, I know it was done because of the `Kebechet` integration, but doesn't `Kebechet` also support the `.txt` file extension for the _requirements_ file?

Fixing it would be simple anyway, just changing the reference in `setup.py` to that of `requirements.in`. But I just wanted to ask if we should revert to using the `txt` extension, it's pretty much the standard.

Either way, I can start a pull-request. Just let me know which path should we take. Excited to start contributing to this project :)

issue_title: sr_watch unsuccessful with some files
issue_body:
was: https://sourceforge.net/p/metpx/bugs/46/
(2016/07/26 Eric Funk)

I have an sr_watch on an 'incoming' directory which is fed various files via SFTP. I have an sr_sarra instance which grabs the files and then deletes them.

Theoretically the incoming directory should be empty or near empty with no old files in it, however I a bunch of old files sitting in the directory that the sr_sarra instance didn't seem to collect.

Looking in the sr_watch log I see the following:

2016-07-26 00:04:23,214 [DEBUG] sr_post cache_load
2016-07-26 00:04:23,214 [DEBUG] sr_poster cache_load
2016-07-26 00:04:23,214 [DEBUG] sr_poster cache_files_set
2016-07-26 00:04:23,214 [DEBUG] cache_file = /local/home/sarra/.cache/sarra/watch/urpx-files/_data_incoming_urp_209715200
2016-07-26 00:04:23,214 [DEBUG] load cache
2016-07-26 00:04:23,214 [DEBUG] sr_post watching
2016-07-26 00:04:23,215 [DEBUG] sr_post posting
2016-07-26 00:04:23,215 [DEBUG] sr_poster post_local_inplace
2016-07-26 00:04:23,215 [DEBUG] sr_poster post_local_file
2016-07-26 00:04:23,215 [DEBUG] sr_config set_sumalgo d
2016-07-26 00:04:23,215 [ERROR] [Errno 13] Permission denied: '//data/incoming/urp/201607260000~~CLOGZPPI,DOPVOL2,18,MPRATE:URP:WWW:RADAR:META'

It seems the sr_watch isn't gettting read permissions on the file and I guess thus can't create the checksum for the post call.

My guess is that sr_watch is seeing the file in between the time the file shows up and before the OS releases it for reading. sr_watch only tries to post a file once before it gives up and moves on and thus the file is left there.

Another error I am seeing in the logs that I am not sure is related or not, are a lot of messages like the following:

2016-07-26 08:40:16,765 [ERROR] File not found //data/incoming/urp/201607260840~~SVRWX,SvrWxParameters,AGL:URP:WHN:RADAR:META

For referencemy sr_watch configuration is as follows:

broker amqp://LOCAL@some-server/
to DSR.WPG

issue_title: Key not set on objects whose key is generated by Riak
issue_body: The HTTP transport offers the ability to let Riak generate a random key. When it does, the Location header reveals the randomly generated key. It would be really nice if this was parsed and the key was set on the RiakObject object.
